message:
fix(test-binding): correct misleading comment for ambiguous predictions

The else branch that sets suggestedAction = "none" is not an edge case -
it's the expected path for ambiguous predictions where neither the result
nor the prediction text has clear polarity indicators.

Updated comment and reason message to accurately reflect this behavior.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

--text-sm: clamp(0.8125rem, 0.775rem + 0.2vw, 0.875rem); --text-base: clamp(1rem, 0.95rem + 0.25vw, 1.125rem); --text-lg: clamp(1.125rem, 1.05rem + 0.4vw, 1.375rem); --text-xl: clamp(1.375rem, 1.25rem + 0.65vw, 1.75rem); --text-2xl: clamp(1.625rem, 1.45rem + 0.9vw, 2.25rem); --text-3xl: clamp(2rem, 1.75rem + 1.25vw, 3rem); --text-4xl: clamp(2.5rem, 2.1rem + 2vw, 4rem); ``` # Line Heights (tighter for headlines) - Body text: 1.6-1.65 - Large text: 1.3-1.5 - Headlines: 1.1-1.2 # Letter Spacing - Small text: slightly loose (0.015em) - Headlines: slightly tight (-0.02em to -0.035em) # Fonts - Sans: Instrument Sans (or Inter as fallback) - Mono: JetBrains Mono (for code, terminal, operators) # Implementation 1. Add font imports to layout.tsx (next/font) 2. Define tokens in globals.css 3. Create utility classes or Tailwind config # Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Fonts load correctly - [ ] Typography scales smoothly across viewport sizes - [ ] Headlines tight, body text comfortable - [ ] Code/mono text uses JetBrains Mono","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:13:08.992044195-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T19:35:45.282026352-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T19:35:45.282026352-05:00","close_reason":"Fluid typography scale added: 8 size tokens using clamp(), line heights, letter spacing, build verified","labels":["design-system","typography","ux","web"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-84o","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-7yz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:13:46.297570969-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-84o","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-1qx","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:21:59.419182619-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"brenner_bot-8b10","title":"Task: Implement Program Storage Layer","description":"# Task: Implement Program Storage Layer ## Objective Create file-based storage for research programs with session management. ## Implementation Details ### File Location `apps/web/src/lib/program-storage.ts` ### Storage Structure ``` .research/ └── programs/ ├── programs.json # Index of all programs └── RP-20251230-cell-fate/ # Per-program directory ├── program.json # Program metadata └── sessions.json # Session order and metadata ``` ### Index File Format ```json { "programs": [ { "id": "RP-20251230-cell-fate", "name": "Cell Fate Determination", "status": "active", "sessionCount": 4, "lastActivityAt": "2025-12-31T15:30:00Z" } ] } ``` ### Program File Format ```json { "id": "RP-20251230-cell-fate", "name": "Cell Fate Determination", "description": "Investigating mechanisms of cell fate decisions...", "researchQuestion": "What determines whether a progenitor cell becomes...", "sessions": ["RS-20251230", "RS-20251231", "RS-20260101", "RS-20260102"], "status": "active", "createdAt": "2025-12-30T09:00:00Z", "updatedAt": "2025-12-31T15:30:00Z", "tags": ["epigenetics", "cell-biology", "development"] } ``` ### Core Functions ```typescript // CRUD operations export async function createProgram( input: Omit ): Promise export async function loadProgram(programId: string): Promise export async function updateProgram( programId: string, updates: Partial ): Promise export async function deleteProgram(programId: string): Promise // Session management export async function addSessionToProgram( programId: string, sessionId: string ): Promise export async function removeSessionFromProgram( programId: string, sessionId: string ): Promise export async function reorderProgramSessions( programId: string, sessionIds: string[] ): Promise // Query operations export async function listPrograms( filter?: { status?: ProgramStatus; tag?: string } ): Promise export async function getActivePrograms(): Promise export async function getProgramForSession( sessionId: string ): Promise ``` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Programs stored in per-program directories - [ ] Index enables fast listing - [ ] Sessions can be added/removed from programs - [ ] Session order is preserved - [ ] Query by status and tags - [ ] Lookup program by session ID ## Dependencies - Task: Define Research Program Schema ## Effort Estimate ~180 lines of code, ~2 hours","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:51:00.537943629-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-31T03:06:17.822786817-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-31T03:06:17.822786817-05:00","close_reason":"Storage layer exists at apps/web/src/lib/storage/program-storage.ts","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-8b10","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-2qyl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:51:33.164614498-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-8b10","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-4buj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:51:33.356683016-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-8b10","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-jcuz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T22:42:19.541683209-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} -{"id":"brenner_bot-8dy3","title":"Feature: Unit Test Coverage (Real Implementations Only)","description":"# Goal Add comprehensive unit tests for ALL untested modules using real implementations. # Philosophy: No Mocks Tests must use actual implementations: - Real parsers, not mock return values - Real DOM rendering via happy-dom - Real state management, not mock stores - Real event handlers, not spy callbacks **ONLY EXCEPTION**: framer-motion mock (already in test-setup.tsx) because animations cause test timing issues. # Modules Requiring Tests ## Priority 2 - Viewer Components (apps/web/src/components/) - `DistillationViewer.tsx` (33KB) - **CRITICAL**: Main document display - `TranscriptViewer.tsx` - Transcript rendering - `QuoteBankViewer.tsx` - Quote bank display - `MetapromptViewer.tsx` - Metaprompt rendering ## Priority 4 - Navigation & Layout - `nav.tsx` (17KB) - Main navigation - `collapsible.tsx` (10KB) - Collapsible sections - `bottom-sheet.tsx` (5.8KB) - Mobile bottom sheet - `comparison-view.tsx` (6.4KB) - Side-by-side comparison - `reading-progress-widget.tsx` (6.0KB) - Reading progress indicator ## Priority 5 - Animation & Utilities - `animated-element.tsx` (6.1KB) - Animation wrapper - `animated-counter.tsx` (2.0KB) - Counter animation - `jargon-text.tsx` (7.8KB) - Jargon inline rendering # Test Patterns to Follow ```typescript // GOOD: Real rendering render(); await userEvent.type(screen.getByRole('searchbox'), 'brenner'); expect(screen.getByText(/§103/)).toBeInTheDocument(); // BAD: Mocked children vi.mock('./SearchResults', () => ({ default: () =>

[ ] Test file size roughly proportional to module complexity","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-30T17:49:44.37797704-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T22:43:54.150283939-05:00","labels":["testing unit-tests no-mocks"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-8dy3","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-vmsb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T22:36:00.86542915-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} +{"id":"brenner_bot-8dy3","title":"Feature: Unit Test Coverage (Real Implementations Only)","description":"# Goal Add comprehensive unit tests for ALL untested modules using real implementations. # Philosophy: No Mocks Tests must use actual implementations: - Objective Create the file-based storage layer for hypotheses with JSON persistence and cross-session indexing. ## Implementation Details ### File Location `apps/web/src/lib/hypothesis-storage.ts` ### Storage Structure ``` .research/ ├── hypotheses/ │ ├── RS-20251230-hypotheses.json # Per-session file │ ├── RS-20251231-hypotheses.json │ └── ... └── hypothesis-index.json # Cross-session lookup ``` ### Per-Session File Format ```json { "sessionId": "RS-20251230", "hypotheses": [ { "id": "H-RS20251230-001", "statement": "Cell fate is epigenetically determined", "origin": "proposed", "state": "active", "createdAt": "2025-12-30T10:00:00Z", "updatedAt": "2025-12-30T10:00:00Z" } ], "lastModified": "2025-12-30T10:00:00Z" } ``` ### Index File Format ```json { "hypotheses": { "H-RS20251230-001": { "sessionId": "RS-20251230", "statement": "Cell fate is epigenetically determined", "state": "active" } }, "lastRebuilt": "2025-12-30T12:00:00Z" } ``` ### Core Functions ```typescript // Read/write operations export async function loadSessionHypotheses(sessionId: string): Promise export async function saveSessionHypotheses(sessionId: string, hypotheses: Hypothesis[]): Promise // Index operations export async function rebuildHypothesisIndex(): Promise export async function getHypothesisById(id: string): Promise export async function searchHypotheses(query: string): Promise // CRUD helpers export async function addHypothesis(hypothesis: Hypothesis): Promise export async function updateHypothesis(id: string, updates: Partial): Promise ``` ## Design Decisions 1. **File-per-session**: Keeps diffs clean, allows partial sync 2. **Separate index**: Fast lookups without loading all files 3. **JSON format**: Human-readable, git-friendly 4. **Async API**: Future-proofs for potential DB backend ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Create `.research/hypotheses/` directory if not exists - [ ] Load/save session hypothesis files - [ ] Maintain cross-session index - [ ] Handle concurrent writes gracefully - [ ] Full-text search across all hypotheses ## Dependencies - Task: Define Hypothesis Zod Schema (for types) ## Effort Estimate ~200 lines of code, ~2 hours","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:43:19.564011089-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-31T02:05:39.723148799-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-31T02:05:39.723148799-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented HypothesisStorage with per-session JSON files, cross-session index, CRUD helpers, state query, search, bulk ops, and unit tests.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-8hv8","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-lm3b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:45:00.520182259-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-8hv8","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-4p5n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:45:00.611263373-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-8hv8","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-jcuz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T22:40:48.223204713-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"brenner_bot-8xu","title":"Reference: agentic_coding_flywheel implementation details","description":"# Reference Implementation Details ## Source github.com/Dicklesworthstone/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup (cloned to /tmp/jargon_reference) This bead documents the key implementation patterns to adapt for BrennerBot. --- ## JargonTerm Schema (`lib/jargon.ts`) ```typescript export interface JargonTerm { term: string; // Display name short: string; // ~1 line for tooltip long: string; // 2-4 sentences, plain English analogy?: string; // "Think of it like..." why?: string; // Why we use it / what problem it solves related?: string[]; // Related term keys } // Dictionary structure export const jargonDictionary: Record = { "term-key": { term: "Term Name", short: "...", long: "...", ... } }; // Lookup helper export function getJargon(termKey: string): JargonTerm | undefined { return jargonDictionary[termKey.toLowerCase().replace(/[\s_]+/g, "-")]; } ``` --- ## Jargon Component (`components/jargon.tsx`) ### Trigger Button Styling ```tsx {/* Content */}
``` ### Animation Configuration ```tsx // Spring animation (respect reduced motion) const prefersReducedMotion = useReducedMotion(); // Tooltip entrance initial: prefersReducedMotion ? { opacity: 0 } : { opacity: 0, y: position === "top" ? 8 : -8, scale: 0.95 } animate: prefersReducedMotion ? { opacity: 1 } : { opacity: 1, y: 0, scale: 1 } transition: prefersReducedMotion ? { duration: 0.12 } : springs.snappy // Sheet entrance initial: prefersReducedMotion ? { opacity: 0 } : { y: "100%" } animate: prefersReducedMotion ? { opacity: 1 } : { y: 0 } transition: prefersReducedMotion ? { duration: 0.12 } : springs.smooth ``` --- ## Glossary Page (`app/glossary/page.tsx`) ### Category System ```tsx type GlossaryCategory = "all" | "shell" | "networking" | "tools" | "concepts"; function categorizeKey(key: string): Exclude { // Regex patterns to auto-categorize based on term key } ``` For BrennerBot, adapt to: ```tsx type GlossaryCategory = "all" | "operators" | "brenner" | "biology" | "bayesian" | "method" | "project"; ``` ### Search Implementation ```tsx function buildSearchable(entry): string { return [ entry.key, entry.term, entry.short, entry.long, entry.analogy ?? "", entry.why ?? "", ...(entry.related ?? []), ].join(" ").toLowerCase(); } // Filter const filtered = entries.filter((entry) => { if (category !== "all" && entry.category !== category) return false; if (query.length === 0) return true; return entry.searchable.includes(query); }); ``` ### Deep Linking ```tsx useEffect(() => { const openFromHash = () => { const raw = window.location.hash.replace(/^#/, ""); if (!raw) return; const key = decodeURIComponent(raw); const target = document.getElementById(key); if (!target) return; // Open
if applicable if (target instanceof HTMLDetailsElement) { target.open = true; } else { const details = target.closest("details"); if (details) details.open = true; } target.scrollIntoView({ behavior: "smooth", block: "start" }); }; openFromHash(); window.addEventListener("hashchange", openFromHash); return () => window.removeEventListener("hashchange", openFromHash); }, []); ``` ### Term Card (expandable details) ```tsx

{entry.term}

#{entry.key} {category}

{entry.short}
{/* Full definition */}

What it means

{entry.long}

{/* Why box (green accent) */} {entry.why && (

Why we use it

{entry.why}

)} {/* Analogy box (primary accent) */} {entry.analogy && (

Think of it like...

{entry.analogy}

)} {/* Related terms */} {entry.related?.length > 0 && (

Related terms

{entry.related.map((key) => ( {key} ))}
)}
``` --- ## Adaptation Notes for BrennerBot ### Styling Changes 1. Replace `oklch` gradient colors with brenner design tokens 2. Use existing CSS animations from globals.css instead of framer-motion springs 3. Match rounded-xl pattern from existing Card component 4. Use existing primary/accent/muted-foreground tokens ### Structural Changes 1. Categories: operators, brenner, biology, bayesian, method, project 2. Add links to transcript §n anchors for biology terms 3. Connect to /corpus/[doc] routes for "learn more" 4. No motion library - use CSS animations or simple transitions ### Missing from Reference (add for BrennerBot) 1. Category icons (different icon per category) 2. Transcript anchor links in "why" sections 3. Operator symbol in term display for operator category --- ## Files to Create 1. `apps/web/src/lib/jargon.ts` - Types + dictionary + helper 2. `apps/web/src/components/jargon.tsx` - Tooltip/sheet component 3. `apps/web/src/app/glossary/page.tsx` - Full glossary page 4. Consider: `apps/web/src/hooks/useReducedMotion.ts` - Motion preference hook","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-30T01:00:21.322247456-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T01:25:12.905543812-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T01:25:12.905543812-05:00","close_reason":"Reference implementation fully documented in earlier session - analyzed jargon.tsx, jargon.ts, and glossary/page.tsx from agentic_coding_flywheel_setup repository","labels":["documentation","glossary","reference"]} {"id":"brenner_bot-8yn","title":"Create base layout with navigation and footer","description":"# Task Build the root layout component with navigation header and footer. # Navigation Header - Logo/brand mark (left) - Main nav links: Corpus, Distillations, Method, Sessions - Theme toggle (optional, dark is default) - Mobile hamburger menu # Footer - Brief description - Links: GitHub repo, Agent Mail, Anthropic/OpenAI/Google - Copyright/attribution note # Layout Structure ```tsx
{children}
